NU Overnight4:00 PM – 11:00 AM next day

NU Overnight

Sleep in a dorm. Eat in the dining hall. Be an NU Eagle for 24 hours.

Pick your date

Register for Friday, October 2, 2026 Swoop Shop

What NU Overnight is really like

Placeholder — video coming soon

What to expect

NU Overnight is the closest you can get to being a student without enrolling. You'll stay in a residence hall with a trained, background-checked student host, eat dinner and breakfast in Sæter, sit in on real morning classes, and experience chapel and campus life after dark. Six dates across the school year — pick the one that fits your schedule.

Your day

Day 1 · 4:00 PM

Check In & Meet Your Host

Get your nametag, drop your bag, and meet the student host you'll be staying with tonight.

Larson Hall Lobby

Day 1 · 4:30 PM

Campus Tour

Student-led walk through campus — trails, chapel, labs, and the spots students actually hang out.

Departs from Larson Hall

Day 1 · 6:00 PM

Dinner at Sæter

Dinner in our campus dining hall with hosts and other overnight guests. Come hungry.

Sæter Dining Hall

Day 1 · 7:30 PM

Evening Activity

Game night, worship night, or a student showcase — depends on the date, always a good time.

University Center

Day 1 · 9:30 PM

Hall Hangout

Snacks, games, and honest conversation with current students in the residence hall lounge.

Residence Hall

Day 1 · 11:00 PM

Lights Out

Settle in with your host. Residence halls are key-card access only, with overnight staff on duty.

Residence Hall

Day 2 · 7:30 AM

Breakfast at Sæter

The most important meal of the day, dining-hall style.

Sæter Dining Hall

Day 2 · 8:30 AM

Class Visit

Sit in on a real NU class in your area of interest.

Academic Buildings

Day 2 · 10:00 AM

Admissions Q&A + Wrap-Up

Final questions with the admissions team, plus next steps if NU feels right.

Larson Hall

Day 2 · 11:00 AM

Pick-Up

Parents pick up students at Larson Hall. Stick around for coffee with our team if you'd like.

Larson Hall

What to bring

Sleeping bag or bedding + pillow (mattress is provided)
Toiletries and a towel
Comfy layers — pajamas, and a jacket for the evening walk
Phone + charger for photos and the campus app
Any personal meds / allergy epi-pens (staff don't carry these)

Dress code

Sleepover Casual

Comfy clothes for day one, pajamas for the night, and something presentable for morning classes.

A note for parents & guardians

Sending your student to sleep on a college campus takes trust. Here's exactly how we keep them safe — and keep you in the loop.

Vetted hosts only

Every student host is background-checked, trained, and supervised by full-time residence life staff.

Secure residence halls

Key-card access only, separated by gender, with overnight staff on duty and a 24/7 campus security line.

Reachable all night

Your student keeps their phone, and our team shares a direct contact number at check-in. You can reach your student at any time.

Simple drop-off & pick-up

Drop off at 4:00 PM Friday, pick up at 11:00 AM Saturday — both at Larson Hall. Free visitor parking.

Thinking about cost? So are we.

Over 90% of our students receive financial aid. Ask us about scholarships during the Saturday wrap-up — or before you even arrive.

Explore scholarships →

Questions & answers

4:00 PM – 11:00 AM next day

Register now — it's free Swoop Shop

No commitment required to register.

Make your name tag

Your name as it will appear on your event badge:

Questions?

Our admissions team replies within one business day.

admissions@northwestu.edu
